title: NOC 40030 – Managers in Social, Community and Correctional Services | GTR Immigration
description: Managers in Social, Community and Correctional Services under NOC 40030 are senior leaders responsible for overseeing social service programs, correctional institutions, community organizations, and non-profit operations across Canada. Classified as TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents a high-level leadership pathway within Canada’s social and public protection sectors. With increasing demand for social welfare programs, rehabilitation services, and community development initiatives, qualified managers remain in steady demand nationwide.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration, education and social and community servicesSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration and educationMinor Group4003 – Managers in social, community and correctional servicesNOC Code40030

duties: Managers in Social, Community and Correctional Services perform some or all of the following:
Plan, organize, direct, and evaluate social service programs
Manage operations of community centres and correctional facilities
Oversee income maintenance, emergency housing, and child welfare programs
Administer NGO and membership organization programs
Liaise with government agencies and media
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in social sciences or administrative discipline (usually required)
Several years of experience in a related occupation such as:Social workerProbation or parole officerCommunity service workerPolicy researcher or program officer
Managers of associations require extensive experience in the relevant industry or field
Senior leadership and management experience are typically essential.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $45,400 to $121,500, depending on province and organizational scope.
The outlook for NOC 40030 is stable, supported by:
Increased funding for social assistance programs
Leadership experience in multi-agency coordination and program budgeting enhances employability.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 40030 are employed in:
Strong employment opportunities exist in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Quebec, Saskatchewan, and northern territories like Nunavut and Northwest Territories.
Leadership roles in social development and correctional systems
Professionals with experience in social services, rehabilitation, or community program leadership are particularly competitive.
pathways: Qualified professionals under NOC 40030 may immigrate to Canada through:
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada as a Manager in Social, Community and Correctional Services.
